finding.contract_claim: L162: parameter `Triage.__init__.executor` — None of the 16 deduplicated call sites pass `executor`. Every caller either passes no optional args (call site 1), passes only `rate_limiter` (call site 2), or passes only `provider` (call sites 5–16). The parameter's only use in the body is the inline expression `executor or ExplorationExecutor(config)`, which always resolves to `ExplorationExecutor(config)` because `executor` is always `None`.
finding.observed_behavior: Remove the `executor` parameter and replace the assignment with `self.executor = ExplorationExecutor(config)` unconditionally. No full if-block is gated — only the inline `or` expression simplifies.
finding.triage_reasoning:
Examined all 14 call sites of Triage(...) across src/osoji/audit.py, scripts/triage_bootstrap.py, and tests/test_triage.py. Every invocation uses only positional `config`, optional `rate_limiter`, and/or keyword `provider`. The keyword `executor` is never passed at any call site.

The `executor` locals in triage_bootstrap.py (lines 168/173) are scoped entirely inside the `explore_one()` async closure and drive the SDK tool-wrapping logic there; they are never forwarded to a Triage constructor.

At L172 the body reads `self.executor = executor or ExplorationExecutor(config)`. Because `executor` is invariably `None`, the right-hand branch always executes. The parameter is unreachable dead code.

All three predicates hold:
- Reality: confirmed — zero call sites supply the argument.
- Significance: the dead parameter exposes a misleading injection surface (implies testability via executor injection, but no test uses it), and leaves dead code in the hot-path constructor.
- Actionability: straightforward one-liner fix.
finding.suggested_fix:
Remove the `executor` keyword parameter from `Triage.__init__` and replace the conditional assignment with an unconditional one:

```python
# Before
def __init__(
    self,
    config: Config,
    rate_limiter: Any | None = None,
    *,
    executor: ExplorationExecutor | None = None,
    provider: Any | None = None,
) -> None:
    ...
    self.executor = executor or ExplorationExecutor(config)

# After
def __init__(
    self,
    config: Config,
    rate_limiter: Any | None = None,
    *,
    provider: Any | None = None,
) -> None:
    ...
    self.executor = ExplorationExecutor(config)
```

No call sites need updating.
